skill.name: playwright-browser
skill.description: Control a Playwright browser from an AI agent workflow to navigate pages, inspect accessibility snapshots, click elements, type text, and capture screenshots.
skill.summary: Automate browser sessions with Playwright CLI commands for navigation, interaction, snapshots, and screenshots.
skill.author: Bind

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives caused by bash examples and inline command references in documentation, not executable Ruby or shell code. The real risk is medium because the skill instructs agents to navigate arbitrary URLs, interact with pages, write screenshots, and preserve cookies and localStorage across sessions. No prompt injection, malicious exfiltration intent, or weak cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed files.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Browser Storage
medium_findings.description: The documentation states that browser data is stored in .playwright-data/ and that cookies and localStorage persist between sessions. This is expected for browser automation, but it can retain authenticated session data if users browse sensitive sites.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The storage behavior is explicitly documented. The risk depends on user browsing choices, so it is a strong security concern but not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Website Navigation And Interaction
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to navigate to supplied URLs and interact with page elements through click and type commands. This is core functionality, but it can submit data to untrusted sites or operate inside authenticated browser sessions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and examples directly support navigation, typing, and clicking. This is legitimate browser automation, but it creates meaningful risk when used on sensitive pages.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The repeated external command findings refer to markdown code fences and inline examples that show how to run Bun-based Playwright commands. I did not find evidence that SKILL.md itself executes shell commands or interpolates untrusted input into a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are visible markdown usage examples, not implementation code. No Ruby backtick execution appears in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Benign Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are example destinations used to demonstrate navigation. They do not show exfiltration endpoints, credential transfer, or hidden network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are normal documentation examples for search and sample navigation. No evidence of suspicious network destinations was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm locations point to the skill description and a markdown table header, not cryptographic code. No evidence found for MD5, SHA-1, encryption, hashing, or signature logic in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain metadata text and markdown table formatting. There is no cryptographic implementation to evaluate at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Browser Context
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents persistent cookies and localStorage. Users should avoid using it with sensitive accounts unless they understand where browser data is stored.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The persistence behavior is directly stated in the documentation. The concern is data retention, not confirmed misuse.

dangerous_patterns.title: Screenshot File Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The screenshot command can write page images to a user-selected path. This may capture sensitive visible content if used on authenticated or private pages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The screenshot output path and full-page capture options are documented. The sensitivity depends on the page being captured.

content.value_statement: AI agents often need to inspect and interact with real web pages, but direct browser control is hard to coordinate. This skill provides documented Playwright CLI commands for navigation, snapshots, clicks, typing, screenshots, and session control.
actual_capabilities: Starts, stops, and checks a persistent Playwright browser session.
Navigates the active browser session to a specified URL.
Reads the current page accessibility tree for element discovery.
Types into page elements and can press a follow-up key such as Enter.
Clicks elements by role, visible text, or CSS selector.
Captures screenshots, including optional full-page screenshots.
limitations: The reviewed package contains documentation and a manifest, but not the referenced implementation files.
Browser storage persists between sessions, so cookies and localStorage require careful handling.
The skill depends on Bun, Playwright, and local browser support being available.
Selectors and accessibility references can fail when pages change their structure.

use_cases.title: Check Web Flows During Development
use_cases.description: Run a local or staging page, inspect its accessibility tree, click controls, and capture screenshots for quick verification.

use_cases.title: Collect Visual Evidence For Issues
use_cases.description: Navigate to a page, reproduce a visible problem, and save screenshots that can be attached to bug reports.

use_cases.title: Explore Web Pages With An Agent
use_cases.description: Let an AI agent inspect page structure and perform simple browser actions while keeping each command explicit.

prompt_templates.title: Open A Page And Inspect It
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a Playwright browser session, navigate to the target URL, and show me the accessibility snapshot.

prompt_templates.title: Search And Capture Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the search page, type my query into the search field, submit it, and save a screenshot of the results.

prompt_templates.title: Verify A Form Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the browser commands to complete this form flow, report each page state, and capture a screenshot after submission.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Multi-Step Interaction
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a persistent session through this multi-step user journey, use snapshots before each action, and summarize selectors, failures, and screenshots.

output_examples.input: Inspect a product page and find the checkout button.
output_examples.output: The page snapshot shows a main product region, quantity controls, and a button named Add to cart. The checkout button is not visible until an item is added.

output_examples.input: Capture evidence after reproducing a layout issue.
output_examples.output: The browser reached the target page, reproduced the narrow layout, and saved a screenshot showing the overlapping navigation labels.

output_examples.input: Test a search form with a short query.
output_examples.output: The search field accepted the query, Enter submitted the form, and the results page loaded after the network became idle.

best_practices: Use snapshots before clicking or typing so actions target visible, expected elements.
Use a separate browser profile for sensitive testing because storage persists across sessions.
Stop the browser session after a workflow to reduce accidental reuse of page state.
anti_patterns: Do not run the browser session against private accounts unless persistent storage is acceptable.
Do not rely on brittle CSS selectors when role or text references are available.
Do not save screenshots of confidential pages into shared project directories.

faq.question: What does this skill control?
faq.answer: It documents commands for controlling a Playwright browser session from an AI agent workflow.

faq.question: Does it support screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents standard and full-page screenshot commands with an optional output path.

faq.question: How does it find page elements?
faq.answer: It recommends using the accessibility snapshot, then targeting elements by role, text, or CSS selector.

faq.question: Does browser state persist?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation states that cookies and localStorage persist in .playwright-data/.

faq.question: Is it safe for authenticated sites?
faq.answer: Use caution. Authenticated cookies, localStorage, typed data, and screenshots can contain sensitive information.

faq.question: What tools are required?
faq.answer: The documented commands use Bun and Playwright, with a local browser available for the session.
